passage: The university library offers several study areas. The silent room is designed for individual reading and does not allow group discussion. The media room contains computers and recording equipment for students working on digital projects. A separate tutorial room can be booked by small groups, but students must reserve it at least one day in advance. During exam periods, the library also opens an overflow hall to provide additional desks. Staff say the mix of spaces is meant to suit both quiet study and collaborative work without letting noise spread through the building.
instructions: Complete the table below. Use NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.

cells.evidence: The silent room is designed for individual reading
cells.explanation: The passage states exactly how the silent room is used.

cells.evidence: The media room contains computers and recording equipment for students working on digital projects.
cells.explanation: The room is used for digital projects.

cells.evidence: A separate tutorial room can be booked by small groups
cells.explanation: The passage says the tutorial room is for small groups.

cells.evidence: the library also opens an overflow hall to provide additional desks
cells.explanation: The overflow hall is opened to provide extra desks.

passage: A city museum studied how visitors moved through one new gallery. First, researchers asked ten people to give short interviews at the exit so they could explain which displays held attention. Next, another group wore lightweight sensors that recorded eye movements while they walked. The team then compared the results with an online survey that measured overall rating and visit length. Finally, staff phoned a small sample of regular visitors two weeks later to ask whether the exhibits were easy to remember. The writer argues that the best research combines quick reactions with later reflection, because immediate praise can hide confusion that only appears after the visit has ended.
instructions: Complete the table below. Use NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.

cells.evidence: asked ten people to give short interviews at the exit so they could explain which displays held attention
cells.explanation: The exit interviews collected visitor reactions.

cells.evidence: wear lightweight sensors that recorded eye movements while they walked
an online survey that measured overall rating and visit length
phoned a small sample of regular visitors two weeks later
cells.explanation: The timing of the follow-up call is given directly.

passage: A coastal energy trial used solar panels by day and stored the extra power in a battery bank for night use. When the weather was windy, the system redirected power into a thermal tank that kept water warm for nearby housing. Engineers also tested software that shifted electricity during peak demand so the grid would not overload. The final report said the most useful feature was not the biggest battery, but the combination of storage types that could balance supply after cloudy days. The writer notes that renewable energy becomes more reliable when different forms of storage support one another rather than competing for the same task.
instructions: Complete the table below. Use NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.

cells.evidence: stored the extra power in a battery bank for night use
cells.explanation: The battery bank stores energy for night use.

cells.evidence: a thermal tank that kept water warm for nearby housing
cells.explanation: The thermal tank keeps water warm.
The software shifts electricity when demand is high.

cells.evidence: could balance supply after cloudy days
cells.explanation: The mixed storage system helps after cloudy days.
